entries.photosynthesis: definition: plant process using sunlight to make food
entries.erosion: definition: wearing away of earth by wind or water
entries.fossil: definition: preserved remains of ancient life
entries.ecosystem: definition: community of living things and environment
entries.habitat: definition: natural home of an organism
entries.species: definition: group of similar organisms that can breed
entries.metamorphic: classify: rock changed by heat and pressure
entries.true__1: true or false: water freezes at zero degrees celsius
entries.false__1: true or false: the sun is a planet
entries.true__2: true or false: humans need oxygen to survive
entries.false__2: true or false: whales are fish

entries.false__3: true or false: the earth is flat
entries.true__4: true or false: gravity pulls objects down
entries.false__4: true or false: sound travels in a vacuum
entries.true__5: true or false: insects have six legs
entries.false__5: true or false: birds have teeth

entries.carbon: definition: element found in all living things
entries.nitrogen: definition: gas that makes up most of air
entries.force: definition: push or pull on an object
entries.matter: definition: anything that has mass and takes up space
entries.mixture: definition: combination of substances not chemically joined
entries.solution: definition: mixture where substances are fully dissolved
entries.respiration: definition: process of releasing energy from food
entries.digestion: definition: breaking down food into nutrients
entries.circulation: definition: movement of blood through the body
entries.evolution: definition: gradual change of species over time
entries.migration: definition: movement from one place to another
entries.pacific: the largest and deepest ocean, covering over 30% of earth’s surface, featuring the mariana trench.
entries.atlantic: the second-largest ocean separating the americas from europe and africa; it is a major hub for global trade routes and history.
entries.indian: the third-largest and warmest ocean, surrounded by asia, africa, and australia, vital for global shipping and tropical ecosystems.
entries.arctic: the smallest and shallowest ocean, located around the north pole and characterized by sea ice, polar bears, and cold temperatures.
entries.southern: the youngest ocean, it circles antarctica, known for its cold, nutrient-rich waters, icebergs, and unique biodiversity.
entries.nucleus: what am i: i control activities inside a cell
entries.ribosome: what am i: i make proteins in a cell
entries.mitochondria: what am i: i produce energy for cells
entries.chloroplast: what am i: i help plants make food
entries.sun: what am i: i am the center of the solar system
entries.moon: what am i: i orbit earth
entries.mars: what am i: i am the red planet
entries.venus: what am i: i am the hottest planet

entries.pond: what am i: i am a small body of still water
entries.desert: what am i: i am very dry with little rain
entries.forest: what am i: i have many trees and animals
